summary: Accepted for source_fidelity. The three PubMed EFetch source snapshots align to the intended PMID source records, and the proposed source, eligibility, study, outcome, result, finding, and risk-of-bias records attach matching source_snapshot_id provenance. The snapshots retain metadata, abstract summaries, locators, and content hashes only; no raw full text or raw XML is stored.

findings.claim_or_issue: Snapshots for PMIDs 31542391, 36857968, and 40010154 have source_id values, PubMed EFetch URL id parameters, and payload_summary.pmid values matching the proposed source records.
findings.why_it_matters: The repair is meant to add durable source provenance without changing source identity; a PMID mismatch would contaminate downstream evidence chains.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep each PubMed EFetch snapshot bound to its matching PMID source.

findings.claim_or_issue: The proposed eligibility, study, outcome, result, finding, and risk-of-bias records each include a source_snapshot_id that matches the record's source_id or source_ids entry.
findings.why_it_matters: The candidate's scope is provenance repair; every updated downstream record needs an unambiguous link back to the durable PubMed snapshot.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; retain the matched source_snapshot_id values on the proposed records.

findings.claim_or_issue: The PubMed EFetch snapshots are explicitly metadata_only artifacts with content hashes, locators, parsed summaries, and raw_storage.stored set to false.
findings.why_it_matters: PubMed EFetch is suitable for source identification and abstract-level provenance, but it should not be treated as reusable full text.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; use separate open full-text or registry snapshots before retaining or citing full-text material.
